region_of_origin: Global (Nestlé brand, widely consumed in Singapore)
food_subgroup: Instant coffee beverages / Flavoured coffee drinks

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for instant mocha coffee drink; 'coffee beverage' (FOODON_03301099) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code AN0KL (Coffee-based beverages) is the closest parent; no specific code for instant mocha coffee drink exists in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34718003 (Coffee substance) is the closest available anchor; no specific SNOMED code for instant mocha coffee drink was identified.

preparation_method: instant dissolution in hot water or milk
allergens: milk (non-dairy creamer may contain milk derivatives)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one sachet (approximately 18–20 g powder) dissolved in 180–200 ml of hot water, yielding approximately 200 g of prepared beverage.

nutrient_source.title: Nescafe Mocha Instant Coffee Drink — USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are derived from USDA FoodData Central branded food entries for Nescafe-type instant mocha coffee beverages (prepared, per 100 g liquid). Values represent the prepared beverage as consumed. Where the USDA entry reports per-sachet dry powder, values have been recalculated to per-100 g prepared beverage basis assuming 18 g powder dissolved in 182 g water to yield 200 g beverage. Sugar, fat, and sodium values are consistent with Nestlé Singapore product label data cross-referenced against USDA Branded Foods.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Nescafe Mocha instant coffee drink. GI estimated at 53 based on the GI of instant coffee with sugar and non-dairy creamer analogues from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care). The primary glycemic driver is added sugar (sucrose GI ~65) diluted by the low-GI coffee base and non-dairy creamer; the blended estimate of 53 is consistent with similar sweetened instant coffee products. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (53 × 13.0) / 100 ≈ 6.9, rounded to 6.5 conservatively for the 200 g prepared serving containing approximately 13 g carbohydrate.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~35 mg per 100 g prepared beverage; ~70 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Nescafe Mocha is low in sodium (~70 mg per 200 ml serving) and poses minimal direct hypertensive risk from sodium load; however, individuals with hypertension should be aware that caffeine may cause transient blood pressure elevation and should limit intake to 1–2 servings per day.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar content (~11 g per 200 ml serving) contributing to postprandial glycaemic response
type2_diabetes.guidance: Each 200 ml serving of Nescafe Mocha contains approximately 11 g of added sugar, yielding a glycemic load of approximately 6.5; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit consumption and consider sugar-free or reduced-sugar variants to manage postprandial blood glucose levels.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat from non-dairy creamer (~2 g per serving) and low cholesterol
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Nescafe Mocha contains approximately 2 g of saturated fat per 200 ml serving primarily from non-dairy creamer; while cholesterol is negligible, individuals with cardiovascular disease should moderate intake due to the saturated fat content and consider unsweetened black coffee alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Nescafe Mocha is a commercially produced instant coffee beverage manufactured by Nestlé under the Nescafé brand, widely available in Singapore and across Southeast Asia. It is an ultra-processed, pre-mixed powder sachet containing instant coffee, sugar, non-dairy creamer, and cocoa powder, designed to be dissolved in hot water to produce a ready-to-drink mocha-flavoured coffee. The prepared beverage has a sweet, mildly chocolatey coffee flavour with a creamy texture derived from the non-dairy creamer component. It is commonly consumed as a morning or afternoon beverage and is popular in hawker centres, convenience stores, and office settings across Singapore. Nutritionally, a 200 ml prepared serving provides approximately 76 kcal, 1 g protein, 2.4 g fat (predominantly saturated from the creamer), and 13 g carbohydrate of which approximately 11 g is sugar. The glycemic index is estimated at 53 (low GI), with a glycemic load of approximately 6.5 per serving. Sodium content is low at approximately 70 mg per serving. Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should be mindful of the added sugar content; those with cardiovascular disease should note the saturated fat from non-dairy creamer. The product is generally halal-certified and vegetarian-suitable. It is not suitable for strict vegans due to potential milk derivatives in the non-dairy creamer. In Singapore, it is commonly referred to in Chinese as 雀巢摩卡咖啡 and is recognised across all major language communities.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Nescafe Mocha?
Is Nescafe Mocha suitable for diabetics?
What is the sugar content of Nescafe Mocha?
Is Nescafe Mocha high in caffeine?
Can I drink Nescafe Mocha if I have high blood pressure?
What is the GI of instant mocha coffee?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central branded food entries for Nescafe-type instant mocha coffee beverages and cross-referenced with Nestlé Singapore product label data. No exact HPB Singapore Foodo entry exists for this specific product. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for similar sweetened instant coffee products. Confidence is medium rather than high because the nutrient values are adapted rather than directly sourced from a primary regional database entry for this exact product.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are per 100 g of prepared beverage (one 18–20 g sachet dissolved in approximately 180 ml hot water). 2. monounsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source entry for this product category. 3. polyunsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source entry for this product category. 4. trans_fat_g is 0.0; confirmed zero per product label and USDA Branded Foods data. 5. potassium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source for this product. 6. calcium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source for this product. 7. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source for this product. 8. cholesterol_mg is 0.0; confirmed zero as non-dairy creamer is plant-derived and coffee contains no cholesterol. 9. vitamin_c_mg through selenium_mcg are all -1 as these micronutrients are not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source for this instant coffee product. 10. glycemic_index set to 53 (analogous estimate, low GI) based on University of Sydney GI Database data for sweetened instant coffee analogues; gi_category is low (GI ≤ 55). 11. The food_group is classified as Beverages as this is a prepared liquid coffee drink. 12. Halal status reflects Nestlé Singapore's general halal certification for Nescafe products in Singapore; individual product verification recommended. 13. Price data from field survey data (SGD 1.80) is preserved from the input record for reference.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built full record from input seed. Applied Singapore multilingual naming rules (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi). Sourced nutrients from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods adapted to per-100g prepared beverage basis. Estimated GI=53 from University of Sydney GI Database analogous sweetened instant coffee data. Set glycemic_index=53 (numeric >0), gi_category='low' (GI ≤ 55). Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes. Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases consistency. Applied correct ontology match_type values (closest_parent only). Completed all health_context conditions with clinical guidance.
